merger_description:
Bolt S.p.A. (**Bolt**), a company owned by AC Europe SCSp managed by affiliates of Ares Management LCC (together with its affiliates, managed funds and vehicles, **Ares**), proposes to acquire joint control by Bolt, over Eni Plenitude S.p.A. Società Benefit (**Plenitude**), a company currently solely controlled by Eni S.p.A., by way of a purchase of shares through the subscription of a capital increase (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition will result in joint control of Plenitude by Ares and Eni S.p.A.

Bolt is an Italian joint stock company and investment entity ultimately controlled by funds managed or advised by affiliates of Ares a global alternative investment manager. Bolt currently holds approximately 20% of the share capital of Plenitude which it acquired from Eni in June 2022. In Australia, Ares' energy interests are held through Windlab Pty Ltd (**Windlab**), jointly owned with Federation Asset Management.

Windlab's portfolio comprises wind development projects in National Electricity Market regions of Queensland, NSW, Victoria, Tasmania and Western Australia (NEM regions), as well as having a development role for one project in Western Australia (which is its only project outside of the NEM). Windlab's activities include the Bungaban project in Queensland (developing project), the Gawara Baya project in North Queensland and the Junction Rivers project in New South Wales.

Plenitude is an integrated energy transition company currently solely controlled by Eni S.p.A.. Globally, Plenitude is active in the production of electricity from renewable sources, in the retail sale of electricity and natural gas to both domestic and non-domestic customers, in the electric mobility sector, and in the provision of energy efficiency services.

In Australia, Plenitude's activities are limited to utility-scale solar generation on the Northern Territory's Darwin–Katherine Interconnected System (outside the NEM regions). Through wholly-owned Australian subsidiaries, Plenitude owns the Katherine solar farm, the Batchelor solar farm and the Manton Dam solar farm with 45 MWAC contracted to the Northern Territory's government-owned electricity retailer Jacana Energy.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Bolt S.p.A. (Bolt), a company owned by AC Europe SCSp managed by
affiliates of Ares Management LCC (together with its affiliates,
managed funds and vehicles, Ares), applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of joint control over Eni
Plenitude S.p.A. Società Benefit (Plenitude) by way of a purchase of
shares through the subscription of a capital increase, as described in
the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Bolt, is an Italian joint stock company and investment
entity ultimately controlled by funds managed or advised by affiliates
of Ares, a global alternative investment manager. Bolt currently holds
approximately 20% of the share capital of Plenitude which it acquired
from Eni in June 2022. In Australia, Ares' energy interests are held
through Windlab Pty Ltd (Windlab), jointly owned with Federation
Asset Management.
Windlab's portfolio comprises wind development projects in National
Electricity Market regions of Queensland, NSW, Victoria, Tasmania
and Western Australia (NEM regions), as well as having a
development role for one project in Western Australia (which is its
only project outside of the NEM). Windlab's activities include the
Bungaban project in Queensland (developing project), the Gawara
Baya project in North Queensland and the Junction Rivers project in
New South Wales.
Plenitude is an integrated energy transition company currently solely
controlled by Eni S.p.A.. Globally, Plenitude is active in the production
of electricity from renewable sources, in the retail sale of electricity
and natural gas to both domestic and non-domestic customers, in
the electric mobility sector, and in the provision of energy efficiency
services.
In Australia, Plenitude's activities are limited to utility-scale solar
generation on the Northern Territory's Darwin–Katherine
Interconnected System (outside the NEM regions). Through wholly-
owned Australian subsidiaries, Plenitude owns the Katherine solar
farm, the Batchelor solar farm and the Manton Dam solar farm with
45 MWAC contracted to the Northern Territory's government-owned
electricity retailer Jacana Energy.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no geographic
horizontal overlap between Ares, its portfolio companies and
Plenitude in the supply of electricity generation in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
